spec_paraphrase: GRAPE: Greedy Redundancy-Aware Pruning for MoE (2604.06542)
paper_verbatim: Does a Global Perspective Help Prune Sparse MoEs Elegantly? (GRAPE = Global Redundancy-Aware Pruning of Experts)
notes: Spec attributes the paper as 'Greedy Redundancy-Aware'; paper's actual expansion is 'Global Redundancy-Aware'. Mild paraphrase but functionally equivalent (greedy is the algorithm, global is the framing) — flagging as info.

spec_paraphrase: Non-uniform per-layer expert budgets using activation-aware CKA similarity; GRAPE's value of non-uniform allocation.
paper_verbatim: GRAPE (Global Redundancy-Aware Pruning of Experts), a global pruning strategy that dynamically allocates pruning budgets based on cross-layer redundancy.
notes: Spec faithfully describes GRAPE's core method.

paper_verbatim: Experiments on Mixtral-8x7B, Mixtral-8x22B, DeepSeek-MoE, Qwen-MoE, and GPT-OSS show that, under the same pruning budget, GRAPE consistently achieves the best average performance.
notes: Spec applies GRAPE to Qwen3-30B (different target model) — paper-internal model list not relevant to spec.

spec_paraphrase: GRAPE's +2.45% peak on Mixtral-8x22B at the 4-expert setting (paper Table 2) demonstrates the value of non-uniform allocation.
paper_verbatim: improves average accuracy over the strongest local baseline by 1.40% on average across pruning settings, with gains of up to 2.45%.
notes: Spec correctly cites 2.45% peak gain (note: paper Table 1, not Table 2 — minor citation slip).

paper_verbatim: y^l = sum_{s_i in S} alpha^l_{s_i} * phi^l_{s_i}(x)
notes: MoE forward formula — assumed model architecture, not algorithm-relevant.

paper_verbatim: Each expert phi^l(.) consists of two linear layers with a GeLU activation in between.
notes: Qwen3 uses SwiGLU, not GeLU — but expert architecture is target-model-defined, not GRAPE-defined.

spec_paraphrase: Paper §3.2 explicitly allows 'CKA, MSE, or other similarity measures' for D^l. CKA is the metric used by Zhang et al. (2025), cited in GRAPE §3.2.
paper_verbatim: D^l can be instantiated using CKA (Davari et al.), mean squared error, or other similarity measures.
notes: Spec selects CKA — sanctioned by paper's open list.
Eq. 2 is the MEAN form used only for visualization (Fig. 1) and Eq. 3 normalization. Spec uses Eq. 11 sum form for the algorithm — correct.

spec_paraphrase: R̃^l = (R^l − min R) / (max R − min R) (Eq. 3 — for logging only)
paper_verbatim: tilde{R}^l = (R^l - min R^{l'}) / (max R^{l'} - min R^{l'})
notes: Spec records Eq. 3 verbatim and clarifies it is for logging only (consistent with paper, where Eq. 3 is for cross-layer comparability/Fig.1).

spec_paraphrase: Uniform pruning wastes budget — some layers have highly redundant experts while others are diverse.
paper_verbatim: expert redundancy varies substantially across layers within the same model.
notes: Spec captures the cross-layer heterogeneity motivation.

paper_verbatim: A = blockdiag(D^1, ..., D^L)
notes: Block-diagonal global matrix is a notational construct; spec works with per-layer D^l directly which is operationally equivalent.

spec_paraphrase: Greedy loop until total surviving experts ≤ global_expert_budget
paper_verbatim: Our objective is to reduce the total number of experts to a target value K < LN by pruning structurally redundant experts.
notes: Spec's `global_expert_budget` = paper's K.

paper_verbatim: argmin_M sum_{i!=j} A_{ij} * M_{ij}
notes: Naive objective explicitly NOT used by paper; the paper itself replaces it with greedy+entropy. Spec correctly does not implement Eq. 5.

paper_verbatim: directly optimizing eq. (5) may lead to a degenerate solution... the pruning budget may be allocated excessively to only a few highly redundant layers, causing severe layer imbalance and even model collapse.
notes: Motivation for entropy safeguard, which spec implements (L168) and floor (D5).

spec_paraphrase: E = −Σ_l (n_l / N_total) × log(n_l / N_total) (cross-layer entropy)
paper_verbatim: p_l = I(D^l (.) M^l) / I(A (.) M)
notes: Spec's n_l/N_total is operationally identical to Eq. 6 (counting form) and Eq. 8 (cluster-fraction form).

spec_paraphrase: E = −Σ_l (n_l / N_total) × log(n_l / N_total)
paper_verbatim: E = - sum_l p_l log p_l
notes: Same Shannon entropy formula.

spec_paraphrase: n_l / N_total cluster-fraction form
notes: Spec's runtime entropy uses cluster_counts[l]/total which is exactly Eq. 8.

spec_paraphrase: E = −Σ_l (n_l / N_total) × log(n_l / N_total)
paper_verbatim: E = - sum_l p_l log p_l
notes: Live entropy in spec follows Eq. 9 (cluster-fraction form).

spec_paraphrase: Ê = E_0 × (1 − γ) (Eq. 10 — entropy threshold)
paper_verbatim: E_b = E (1 - gamma)
notes: Spec records Eq. 10 verbatim.

spec_paraphrase: γ=0.1 (project-chosen, not from paper)
paper_verbatim: where gamma in [0,1] is an entropy tolerance parameter... A larger gamma allows more imbalance across layers during pruning.
notes: Paper gives no default for γ. Spec pins γ=0.1; D3 row in §12 documents this with paper-internal justification (paper leaves it unspecified, 0.1 chosen empirically). γ=0.1 ∈ [0,1] so within paper's allowed range.

spec_paraphrase: R^l = Σ_{i≠j} D^l_{ij} (Eq. 11 — sum, not mean)
paper_verbatim: R^l = sum_{i != j} D^l_{ij}
notes: Extractor confirmed: sum semantics match the spec annotation. Spec is verbatim accurate.

spec_paraphrase: Eq. 11 — sum, not mean
paper_verbatim: [Eq. 2 mean] vs [Eq. 11 sum]
notes: Spec explicitly highlights the sum-not-mean distinction critical for layer selection.

spec_paraphrase: GRAPE Algorithm 1 (entropy-aware greedy merge with restart)
paper_verbatim: Require: Similarity blocks {D^l}, target experts K, entropy tolerance gamma. Ensure: Clusters s.t. sum |C^l| = K
notes: Spec's stage1_grape implements this signature.

spec_paraphrase: Initialize each expert as its own cluster. Compute per-layer redundancy R^l = Σ_{i≠j} D^l_{ij}.
notes: Spec init matches Algorithm 1 line 1 exactly.

spec_paraphrase: Set entropy threshold Ê = E_0 × (1 − γ) where E_0 is the initial cross-layer entropy.
paper_verbatim: E <- Entropy({C^l}), E_b <- E(1-gamma), F <- empty
notes: Matches Alg 1 line 2.

spec_paraphrase: Greedy loop until total surviving experts ≤ global_expert_budget
paper_verbatim: while sum_l |C^l| > K do
notes: Loop condition matches.

spec_paraphrase: If all layers frozen → restart (unfreeze all)
paper_verbatim: if F = {1,...,L} then F <- empty // restart
notes: Restart preserved.

spec_paraphrase: Pick l* = argmax R^l among unfrozen layers above their floor
paper_verbatim: l* <- argmax_{l not in F} R^l
notes: Spec adds floor constraint (min_experts_per_layer = N//2) to argmax — paper has no floor. D5 sanctions this with the rationale of bounding compression to the regime where paper results hold.

spec_paraphrase: Pick (i*, j*) = argmax D^{l*}_{ij} (most similar pair)
paper_verbatim: (i*, j*) <- argmax_{i!=j} D^{l*}_{ij}
notes: Pair selection matches.

spec_paraphrase: Merge: zero out j*'s row/column in D^{l*}, update R^{l*}; decrement cluster_counts[l*]
paper_verbatim: C^{l*} <- Union(C^{l*}, i*, j*)
notes: Cluster union performed; matches line 8.

spec_paraphrase: Zeros the absorbed expert's entire row and column in D^l
notes: D4 explicitly documents this deviation: spec zeros full row/col of absorbed expert (vs paper's pair-only zeroing) to prevent stale similarity values from distorting future R^l and layer selection.

spec_paraphrase: Recomputes R^l from updated matrix (after zeroing the absorbed expert's full row/column)
notes: Coupled to D4's full row/col zeroing: since the entire row/col is zeroed, R^l is recomputed from the updated matrix rather than incrementally subtracting 2D[i*,j*]. Spec note L187 explains the paper's incremental update is consistent with the sum form but leaves stale entries.

spec_paraphrase: Recompute global entropy after each merge (used for freeze decision)
paper_verbatim: E <- Entropy({C^l})
notes: Entropy update matches line 11.

spec_paraphrase: If entropy drops below Ê → freeze layer l*
paper_verbatim: if E < E_b then F <- F union {l*}
notes: Freeze logic matches lines 12-13.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 1 outputs per-layer expert budgets (cluster sizes)
paper_verbatim: return {C^l}
notes: Spec returns budget = |C^l| per layer; functionally equivalent.

spec_paraphrase: Phase D: GRAPE Algorithm 1 (entropy-aware greedy merge with restart)
paper_verbatim: GRAPE performs one-shot entropy-aware greedy pruning with restart
notes: One-shot greedy nature preserved; no fine-tuning loop in Stage 1.

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x22B consist of 56 layers... 8 experts, 2 activated.
notes: Target model in spec is Qwen3-30B (40 MoE layers, 256 experts, top-8); paper's Mixtral architecture not used.

paper_verbatim: Deepseek-MoE-16B contains 27 MoE layers, each comprising 64 private experts and 2 shared experts.
notes: Different model not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: GPT-oss has 24 MoE layers, 4 of 32 experts activated.
notes: Different model not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: We compare GRAPE with four recent MoE pruning approaches.
notes: Paper-internal benchmark, not spec concern.

spec_paraphrase: Uniform pruning wastes budget — GRAPE's value of non-uniform allocation.
paper_verbatim: All baseline methods perform uniform pruning... our method maintains the same overall pruning budget but adaptively determines the number of experts to prune in each layer.
notes: Spec captures the uniform-vs-global contrast that motivates GRAPE.

paper_verbatim: we disable all fine-tuning stages for all methods.
notes: Paper's fair-comparison protocol; spec's full pipeline does include Router KD and Stage 6 evals (post-pipeline), but Stage 1 GRAPE itself is one-shot weight-space — no fine-tune at this stage.

paper_verbatim: We set the reasoning effort of GPT-OSS to medium and randomly sample 1000 examples from MMLU.
notes: GPT-OSS-specific eval protocol; spec evaluates Qwen3-30B.

paper_verbatim: For Mixtral and DeepSeek-MoE, the models are prompted to directly generate the final answer.
notes: Paper-specific eval protocol.

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x22B baseline avg 70.4.
notes: Reference table value not used in spec.

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x22B GRAPE 2e/4e: avg 68.2/62.6.
notes: Paper-internal benchmark.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-MoE GRAPE 2e/4e: avg 49.8/49.5.
notes: Paper-internal benchmark.

paper_verbatim: GPT-OSS GRAPE 2e/4e: avg 90.3/89.5.
notes: Paper-internal benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: GRAPE's +2.45% peak on Mixtral-8x22B at the 4-expert setting (paper Table 2)
paper_verbatim: On Mixtral-8x22B, GRAPE reaches average accuracies of 68.2 (2e) and 62.6 (4e), outperforming the strongest local baseline by 1.79% and 2.45%, respectively.
notes: Spec correctly cites +2.45% peak. Note: spec says 'Table 2' but it's actually Table 1 (Table 2 is GPT-OSS). Minor table-citation slip.

paper_verbatim: On DeepSeek-MoE... +1.84% / +1.43%.
notes: Specific gains not cited.
Specific gains not cited.

paper_verbatim: severe imbalance in layerwise redundancy can cause global pruning to collapse the model.
notes: Spec mitigates via floor constraint (D5) and entropy safeguard, but does not explicitly cite the DeepSeek collapse warning. Implicit motivation.

paper_verbatim: additional experimental results on Mixtral-8x7B and Qwen-MoE.
notes: Appendix content, not spec-relevant.

paper_verbatim: Global Greedy = GRAPE in figures.
notes: Naming alias, no spec impact.

paper_verbatim: On Mixtral-8x7B, the advantage of Global Greedy becomes particularly clear under 4e pruning.
notes: Appendix observation.

paper_verbatim: On QwenMoE... Global Greedy still provides consistently strong retention.
notes: Appendix observation.

paper_verbatim: four types: visiting frequency-guided, router-guided, search-based, feature-based.
notes: Background taxonomy, not spec-relevant.

spec_paraphrase: Uniform pruning wastes budget — some layers have highly redundant experts while others are diverse.
paper_verbatim: these methods typically allocate the pruning budget uniformly across all layers, ignoring inter-layer variation in sparsity.
notes: Motivation captured.

paper_verbatim: Cross-layer redundancy of different MoE models.
notes: Figure caption.

paper_verbatim: the final layer of DeepseekMoE exhibit disproportionately high redundancy.
notes: DeepSeek-specific observation; spec uses Qwen3.

spec_paraphrase: Entropy-aware greedy with freeze when entropy drops below threshold (implicitly relies on this interpretation).
paper_verbatim: A larger entropy indicates that the retained experts are distributed more evenly across layers.
notes: Interpretation implicit in spec's freeze logic at L168.

spec_paraphrase: Initialize each expert as its own cluster.
paper_verbatim: each cluster corresponds to one retained expert after merging.
notes: Cluster semantics preserved (note: spec consumes |C^l| as per-layer expert budget for Stage 2, not as a deletion plan — operationally consistent with the paper's cluster→retained-expert mapping).

spec_paraphrase: Phase D: GRAPE Algorithm 1 (entropy-aware greedy merge with restart)... If entropy drops below Ê → freeze layer.
paper_verbatim: GRAPE uses global entropy as a safeguard in a greedy pruning procedure.
notes: Spec uses freeze/restart pattern, not gradient-based regularization — matches paper.

spec_paraphrase: Non-uniform per-layer expert budgets... GRAPE jointly determines budgets under a global expert budget K.
paper_verbatim: our approach jointly determines expert merging across all layers under a unified pruning budget.
notes: Unified-budget formulation captured.

paper_verbatim: DEK is the strongest local baseline on Mixtral-8x22B.
notes: Paper-internal benchmark detail.

paper_verbatim: Unpruned Mixtral-8x22B avg 70.4.
notes: Reference value.

paper_verbatim: Unpruned DeepSeek-MoE avg 50.0.
notes: Reference value.

paper_verbatim: Unpruned GPT-OSS avg 90.0.
notes: Reference value.

paper_verbatim: 2e/4e = number of experts to prune per layer.
notes: Paper notation; spec uses absolute global budget K instead.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek's 2 shared experts always active; GRAPE prunes among 64 private experts.
notes: DeepSeek-specific architecture detail; not relevant to Qwen3 (no shared experts).
